methodsAttempted.query: site:bath.ac.uk University of Bath generative AI assessment policy students artificial intelligence
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official Bath pages, Teaching Hub guidance, Library guidance, and official blog/news pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:teachinghub.bath.ac.uk University of Bath GenAI assessment ethical legal Copilot
methodsAttempted.notes: Official Learning and Teaching Hub pages contained the strongest public staff and assessment guidance.
Followed related links to Two-Lane FAQ, Copilot introduction, staff ethical/legal guidance, and academic integrity statement.
Canonicalized final URLs and rejected official pages that were robots-blocked, inaccessible, news-only, or duplicative.

artifacts.summary: Official University of Bath source discovery found public Teaching Hub assessment guidance, staff GenAI legal/ethical guidance, Microsoft Copilot guidance, and the Academic Integrity Statement. Library, blog, and news-style pages were not used as policy evidence where blocked, inaccessible, or not primary guidance.

stopConditions: Use official University of Bath public sources only.
Stop after source-backed staged candidate claims are prepared and validated.
Do not bypass login, robots, WAF, or browser-check restrictions.
Do not write raw HTML, PDF, screenshots, logs, or browser profiles to the repository.

artifacts.claimText: University of Bath assessment guidance uses ABC GenAI categories: Type A means GenAI is not permitted, Type B means optional assistive use for specific defined processes, and Type C means GenAI is integral to the assessment.
artifacts.normalizedValue: ABC assessment categories: A not permitted, B optional assistive use, C integral use

artifacts.claimText: University of Bath teaching guidance says that from 2026/27 it will move to a two-lane approach, with a closed lane where GenAI must not be used and an open lane where GenAI engagement is necessary or optional.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Two-lane assessment model from 2026/27: closed no GenAI, open optional or necessary GenAI engagement

artifacts.title: University of Bath staged AI policy evidence draft
artifacts.summary: Staged draft summarizes University of Bath public guidance on GenAI assessment categories, transition to the Two-Lane model, Microsoft Copilot support and data safeguards, confidential-data limits, academic integrity declarations, summative feedback judgement, and open-lane misuse framing.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This staged bundle is based only on public official University of Bath sources accessible during the run; it does not publish canonical claims.
Official Library guidance was not used as claim evidence because robots.txt blocks ChatGPT-style agents, and the official student blog page was treated as inaccessible by direct HTTP.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
